tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: First Person with Down Syndrome Crosses Ironman Finish Line
stories.summary: Chris Nikic made history as the first person with Down syndrome to complete a full Ironman, guided by Dr. Tommy Martin who changed his race plans to help him cross the finish line.

stories.headline: From Brink of Death to World Cup Glory: Raul Jimenez's Tearful Return
stories.summary: At 35, Raul Jimenez scored his first World Cup goal in front of 80,000 fans at the Azteca Stadium, pointing to the sky in honor of his late father who died in March.

stories.headline: A Father's Love, a Son's Dream: Basketball Champion at 13
stories.summary: Thirteen-year-old Apei excels at basketball with a crutch his father handmade, then received a donated prosthetic to soar even higher.

stories.headline: Heart Patients Get Relief: Stamp-Sized Ultrasound Heals Without Surgery
stories.summary: MIT engineers created a postage-stamp-sized ultrasound sticker pacemaker that safely restored normal heart rhythms in rats without invasive surgery.

stories.headline: Free Store Opens Doors—and Hearts—to Neighbors in Need
stories.summary: Volunteers created a welcoming sanctuary where struggling neighbors receive food, dignity, and the human kindness that sustains us all.

stories.headline: Teenagers' Hands Answer Prayers: Roofs Fixed, Families Restored
stories.summary: Teenagers from Richmond spent four days repairing homes for low-income families on the Eastern Shore, led by 72-year-old retired priest Churchill Pinder.

stories.headline: Wind Powers Farmers' Future: Minnesota's Green Ammonia Plant Breaks Free
stories.summary: Minnesota farmers now grow their own fertilizer using wind turbines, cutting costs and fossil fuel dependence in one bold stroke.

stories.headline: Lost Flower Blooms Again: 130-Year Mystery Solved in Turkish Hills
stories.summary: Nature enthusiast Ercan Eftelioglu spotted the 'Lost Amasya tulip' thriving in Turkish hills after 130 years, rediscovering a species last seen in 1896.

stories.headline: Fusion Milestone: Germany Holds Plasma Steady for 43 Seconds
stories.summary: Germany's Wendelstein 7-X held fusion plasma steady for 43 seconds, stretching the record by an order of magnitude and bringing limitless clean power closer to reality.

stories.headline: MIT's Gel Coating Delivers Medicine Where It's Needed Most
stories.summary: MIT researchers developed a gel coating that lets doctors target powerful medicines directly to the esophagus, sparing patients invasive procedures.
